notre_dame_montreal.architecture_style: Gothic Revival (James O'Donnell); interior wood carving
notre_dame_montreal.description: Azure vault, starry ceiling, and altarpiece astonish visitors; Céline Dion wedding made global headlines.
facts: O'Donnell converted to Catholicism before death and is buried in the crypt.
Aura sound-and-light shows run on a separate ticket many evenings.
notre_dame_montreal.history: Protestant city fathers eventually welcomed Irish Catholic migration; the basilica became a national historic site.
notre_dame_montreal.significance: The most visited ecclesiastical interior in North America for many years.

old_port_montreal.year_built: Port activity from New France; redevelopment from 1990s
old_port_montreal.architecture_style: Historic quays, contemporary promenades and pavilions
old_port_montreal.description: Cycling paths, the Montréal observation wheel, winter skating, and ferries frame views of Habitat 67.
facts: Jacques Cartier landed nearby in 1535 at the indigenous village of Hochelaga.
Canada Steamship Lines warehouses once lined these wharves.
old_port_montreal.history: Shipping moved downstream to modern terminals; heritage tourism reclaimed the stone embankment.
old_port_montreal.significance: Waterfront hinge between convention towers and colonial street grid.

mount_royal_lookout.description: Picnic crowds and buskers gather above the downtown forest bowl; winter cross-country trails start nearby.
facts: Frederick Law Olmsted planned the summit reserve McGill helped protect.
Montreal takes its name from the French mont royal visible from the river.
mount_royal_lookout.history: Civic romanticism preserved a bald summit while suburbs climbed flanks under later debates.
mount_royal_lookout.significance: The obligatory panorama for understanding Montreal geography.

montreal_biosphere.address: 160 Tour de Ville, Île Sainte-Hélène, Parc Jean-Drapeau
montreal_biosphere.architecture_style: Buckminster Fuller geodesic dome on mast ring
montreal_biosphere.description: Environment-focused exhibitions on water, climate, and Great Lakes ecology inside the acrylic cells.
facts: Fire damaged the skin in 1976; newer membranes reinterpret Fuller's intent.
Operates under Environment and Climate Change Canada direction.
montreal_biosphere.history: United States fair presence became a rare intact Fuller dome in public use.
montreal_biosphere.significance: Expo 67 icon repurposed for sustainability storytelling.

olympic_stadium_montreal.description: Guided tours of vestiges, intermittent sports and concerts; inclined tower observation reopened in phases.
facts: 1976 Summer Games left massive municipal debt and leaky fabric roof sag.
The Expos moved out; MLS soccer explored reuse more than once.
olympic_stadium_montreal.history: Mayor Drapeau's mega-project reshaped east-end industrial lots into a global stage.
olympic_stadium_montreal.significance: Love-it-or-fix-it symbol of Olympic ambition and maintenance reality.

jean_talon_market.year_built: 1933 art déco market buildings; open-air sheds expanded
jean_talon_market.architecture_style: Public market halls and seasonal canopy stalls
jean_talon_market.description: Quebec produce, cheese makers, florists, and fishmongers draw cooks from across the island.
facts: Named for New France intendant Jean Talon.
Metro Jean-Talon connects blue and orange lines underground.
jean_talon_market.history: Agricultural wharf markets moved inland with rail and Italian immigrant vendor culture.
jean_talon_market.significance: Busiest public food market in North America by many counts.

mcgill_campus.address: 845 Sherbrooke Street West, downtown university precinct
mcgill_campus.description: McCord Museum neighbors Roddick Gates; geese haunt Lower Field toward the mountain.
facts: James McGill's estate bequest seeded the Anglican institution.
Research hospital network partners with French-language université networks citywide.
mcgill_campus.history: Scottish merchant philanthropy met colonial middle-class education demand.
mcgill_campus.significance: English Canada's research flagship pressed against French-speaking civic life.

saint_joseph_oratory.architecture_style: Italian Renaissance revival domes on escarpment
saint_joseph_oratory.description: Votive steps, Brother André tomb lines, and city-wide copper dome views reward steep approaches.
facts: Largest church dome in Canada by interior volume tradition.
Pilgrims leave crutches as ex-votos in side chapels.
saint_joseph_oratory.history: Saint André Bessette's healing cult grew a hillside campus of hospitality buildings.
saint_joseph_oratory.significance: Quebec spirituality landmark visible from airliners on clear days.

place_jacques_cartier.address: Old Montreal pedestrian spine toward the river
place_jacques_cartier.architecture_style: French colonial grid plaza with Nelson column 1809
place_jacques_cartier.description: Terraces, street performers, and calèches converge; terraces spill from 19th-century bank buildings.
facts: Nelson's Column predates Trafalgar Square's pillar as imperial trophy.
Renamed after cartographer Jacques Cartier in 1847.
place_jacques_cartier.history: Public punishment site became genteel promenade as port wealth dressed stone façades.
place_jacques_cartier.significance: Social theater between city hall, river cruise docks, and tourist retail.

montreal_museum_fine_arts.address: 1380 Sherbrooke Street West, Golden Square Mile
montreal_museum_fine_arts.year_built: 1912 Beaux-Arts wing; Dessaules pavilions 1991; Claire and Marc Bourgie 2011
montreal_museum_fine_arts.description: World cultures, Canadian masters, decorative arts, and Rodin bronzes span linked pavilions.
facts: William B. Angus gift seeded the encyclopedic collections.
Underground passage connects to Concordia EV building for students.
montreal_museum_fine_arts.history: Anglophone merchant millionaires donated mansions and paintings atop the escarpment.
montreal_museum_fine_arts.significance: Montreal's flagship art museum on the Main divide's wealthy slope.

jacques_cartier_bridge.address: St. Lawrence linking Montreal Island to Longueuil
jacques_cartier_bridge.description: Cycling path and traffic share lanes; seasonal light pulse signals civic mood.
facts: Traffic sometimes closes for fireworks competition weekends.
Named for French navigator, distinct from Cartier Bridge in other cities.
jacques_cartier_bridge.history: Automobile boom required new south shore link before Jacques-Cartier tunnel choke.
jacques_cartier_bridge.significance: The glowing spine of summer nights above the fleuve.

montreal_city_hall.description: Copper mansard roof and ceremonial balcony where De Gaulle shouted Vive le Québec libre in 1967.
facts: Interior marriage halls and council chamber can be toured by appointment.
Fronts Place Jacques-Cartier with symmetrical pavilions.
montreal_city_hall.history: Victorian francophone civic pride rebuilt in stone after the great fire.
montreal_city_hall.significance: Seat of North America's largest French-speaking municipality.

underground_city_montreal.address: RESO network: downtown core roughly bounded by Peel–Berri
underground_city_montreal.description: Thirty-plus kilometers link metro, malls, offices, and universities—winter survival infrastructure.
facts: Operated by multiple private owners with imperfect wayfinding signage.
Expands with each new tower podium plate.
underground_city_montreal.history: Hotel tunnel experiments became a commercial response to sub-zero wind chill.
underground_city_montreal.significance: Defines Montreal workday circulation distinct from sunbelt downtowns.

habitat_67.description: Private condos now; guided architecture tours occasionally open stacked terraces toward the river.
facts: Modular concrete units factory-cast then crane-assembled.
Symbol of Canadian pavilion optimism when housing policy still mattered aesthetically.
habitat_67.history: Student thesis scaled to world's fair; maintenance costs tempered imitation.
habitat_67.significance: Most photographed Brutalist experiment in the country.

clock_tower_old_port.address: Old Port quay near Quai de l'Horloge
clock_tower_old_port.description: Climb seasonally for river-trade views; sailors' memorial plaques at the base.
facts: Commemorates merchant mariners lost in the First World War.
Still signals with Beaux-Arts lantern though navigation is electronic.
clock_tower_old_port.history: Harbour commission beautified working waterfront before containerization moved cargo.
clock_tower_old_port.significance: Vertical counterpoint to flat quays and Ferris wheel.

parc_jean_drapeau.address: Île Sainte-Hélène and Île Notre-Dame, St. Lawrence
parc_jean_drapeau.architecture_style: Landscape park, F1 circuit, aquatic complexes
parc_jean_drapeau.description: Osheaga, F1 Canadian Grand Prix, beach at Jean-Doré, and winter cross-country share the islands.
facts: Artificial Notre Dame island built from Metro tunnel spoil.
Lawrence pond site of rowing and beach volleyball in warmer months.
parc_jean_drapeau.history: Industrial and fairground uses yielded weekend ecology labs and event venue cash flow.
parc_jean_drapeau.significance: Montreal's festival island escape minutes from downtown.

parc_jeanne_mance.address: Between Mount Royal Avenue and Duluth, eastern flank of Mount Royal Park
parc_jeanne_mance.description: Frisbee, softball, and winter tubing hills face downtown towers above the treeline.
facts: Honors cofounder of Hôtel-Dieu hospital and colonial educator.
Separates student Plateau streets from mountain forest trails.
parc_jeanne_mance.history: Buffer zone saving park edge from row-house creep upward.
parc_jeanne_mance.significance: Neighborhood lung between Plateau residents and the mountain.

square_saint_louis.address: Between Saint Louis Square Street and Laval, Plateau
square_saint_louis.year_built: Victorian square 1876; Victorian row houses ring
square_saint_louis.description: Fountain, tennis court scale lawn, and colorful façades appear on every Montreal rom-com poster.
facts: Former cholera cemetery land turned genteel speculative housing.
Émile Nelligan's childhood home overlooks benches.
square_saint_louis.history: Francophone bourgeoisie showcase block near Saint-Denis café axis.
square_saint_louis.significance: Architectural avatar of le Plateau lifestyle.

marche_bonsecours.address: 350 Saint Paul Street East, Old Montreal
marche_bonsecours.architecture_style: Tetrastyle portico silver dome public building
marche_bonsecours.description: Tourist crafts and exhibition rentals replaced wholesale meat; the dome still punctuates skyline walks.
facts: Briefly housed city hall and Exhibition-era party events.
Named for adjacent Notre-Dame-de-Bon-Secours chapel sailors favored.
marche_bonsecours.history: Canal-era provisioning hub became heritage shell for culture industries.
marche_bonsecours.significance: Silver cupola instantly reads as Old Montreal postcard.

montreal_botanical_garden.year_built: 1931 founding; pavilions and Chinese garden later
montreal_botanical_garden.description: Thirty outdoor gardens plus insectarium next door on the same metro stop.
facts: Sister institution with Université de Montréal plant research.
First Nations garden and Japanese pavilion deepen interpretation.
montreal_botanical_garden.history: Depression-era civic beautification survived near Olympic stadium shadow.
montreal_botanical_garden.significance: One of the world's great temperate botanical collections.

pointe_a_calliere.description: Excavated foundations of Fort Ville-Marie; multimedia birth-of-Montreal narrative for schools.
facts: Built where Samuel de Champlain planted 1642 settlement.
Collects urban archaeology as the city digs metro and condo foundations.
pointe_a_calliere.history: Anniversary politics of 1992 turned preservation into flagship interpretation center.
pointe_a_calliere.significance: Ground-zero museum for colonial myth and critique under cobbles.

planetarium_rio_tinto_alcan.address: 4801 Pierre-de Coubertin Avenue, Espace pour la vie campus
planetarium_rio_tinto_alcan.architecture_style: Sustainable showcase with domed theaters (Cardin Julien et al.)
planetarium_rio_tinto_alcan.description: Dual domes frame astronomical shows beside Olympic velodrome shell that houses the Biodôme.
facts: Part of Space for Life cluster with botanic garden and insectarium.
LEED-NC certification stressed energy recovery.
planetarium_rio_tinto_alcan.history: Old Dow planetarium handoff to modern immersion projectors.
planetarium_rio_tinto_alcan.significance: Family astronomy anchor east of downtown.

atwater_market.year_built: 1933 art déco market hall near Lachine Canal
atwater_market.description: Indoor cheese and produce with summer terraces facing canal bike path revitalization.
facts: Named for Edwin Atwater, municipal father.
Canal-side gentrification Loft district walks start here.
atwater_market.history: Industrial worker neighborhood market survived deindustrialization through foodie tourism.
atwater_market.significance: West-end rival to Jean-Talon's ethnic scale.

notre_dame_neiges_cemetery.description: One million interments in tree-lined sections; winter funerals demand snow crews.
facts: Quebec cultural figures from hockey to politics rest here.
Adjacent Protestant Mount Royal Cemetery predates and partners for sightlines.
notre_dame_neiges_cemetery.history: Parish cemeteries overflowed; bishops secured mountain acreage.
notre_dame_neiges_cemetery.significance: Francophone spiritual geography mirrored in stone memorial typography.

montreal_insectarium.address: 4581 Sherbrooke Street East, Espace pour la vie
montreal_insectarium.description: Live colonies, pinned taxonomy walls, and citizen science programs promote arthropod literacy.
facts: Moved closer to botanical garden metro spine after redesign.
Butterfly emergence chamber remains a child highlight.
montreal_insectarium.history: Pioneer institution grew from entomologist Georges Brossard evangelism.
montreal_insectarium.significance: Rare standalone insect museum in North America.

biodome_montreal.address: 4777 Pierre-de Coubertin Avenue, Olympic velodrome shell
biodome_montreal.description: Laurentian forest, Gulf of St. Lawrence, sub-Antarctic islands, and Amazon greenhouse simulate biomes.
facts: Penguins and puffins anchor cold galleries.
Closed for years during climate-control overhaul.
biodome_montreal.history: Post-Olympic shell avoided demolition by turning fair biology spectacle inward.
biodome_montreal.significance: Climate storytelling inside Montreal's Olympic concrete legacy.

eglise_saint_patrick_montreal.description: Timber hammer-beam roof, stained glass, and Stations carved by Irish immigrants' donations.
facts: Served Famine-era Irish laborers building Victoria Bridge.
Declared national historic site for bilingual congregational continuity.
eglise_saint_patrick_montreal.history: Loyalties to Rome and Dublin intertwined in cholera and railway boom years.
eglise_saint_patrick_montreal.significance: English Catholic counter-story to francophone parish geography.

rue_saint_paul_old_montreal.description: Galleries, hotels, and restaurant patios thread between Place d'Youville and Bonsecours.
facts: Oldest paved commercial axis still reads medieval widths in places.
Named after Paul de Chomedey, Maisonneuve companion.
rue_saint_paul_old_montreal.history: Fur trade counting houses became loft boutiques after port decline.
rue_saint_paul_old_montreal.significance: Pedestrian humidity of Old Montreal condensed into one stroll.

chalet_du_mont_royal.address: 1196 Camillien-Houde Way, Mount Royal Park summit
chalet_du_mont_royal.description: Terrace views sweep from downtown to St. Lawrence; snack bar and seasonal events draw tour buses and hikers.
facts: Built during unemployment-relief programs between the wars.
Often paired in photos with the downtown skyline after dark.
chalet_du_mont_royal.history: City Beautiful ideals capped Olmsted's summit clearings with public hospitality space.
chalet_du_mont_royal.significance: Social heart of the mountain beside the Kondiaronk lookout path network.

parc_la_fontaine.address: Roy Street East between Sherbrooke and Mont-Royal, Plateau
parc_la_fontaine.year_built: 1908 park layout on former military drills
parc_la_fontaine.architecture_style: English picturesque lake and open-air theatre
parc_la_fontaine.description: Rowboats in summer, cross-country ski loops in winter; Théâtre de Verdure hosts francophone shows.
facts: Named for Louis-Hippolyte La Fontaine, reform politician.
Lake fed once by city aqueduct experiments.
parc_la_fontaine.history: Military grounds became hygiene reform playground for crowded tenement wards.
parc_la_fontaine.significance: Plateau democratic backyard bigger than British royal parks nearby.

place_des_arts_montreal.address: 175 Sainte-Catherine Street West, Quartier des spectacles
place_des_arts_montreal.architecture_style: International Style cultural complex; plaza festivals
place_des_arts_montreal.description: OSM, Opéra de Montréal, jazz festival stages, and Franco book fair tents occupy the superblock.
facts: Underground malls tie to metro and new concert hall across street.
LED façades on neighboring towers sync during Nuit blanche.
place_des_arts_montreal.history: Jean Drapeau modernism clustered high culture to rival other Canadian metropolises.
place_des_arts_montreal.significance: Winter and summer performing-arts generator for francophone creation.

grande_bibliotheque_banq.address: 475 De Maisonneuve Boulevard East, Latin Quarter
grande_bibliotheque_banq.architecture_style: Contemporary glass stack with forest interior metaphor
grande_bibliotheque_banq.description: BAnQ national library of Quebec collections plus public workstations facing lively Berri corner.
facts: Consolidates Bibliothèque nationale du Québec with Grande Bibliothèque role.
Underground links toward metro and UQAM campus.
grande_bibliotheque_banq.history: Quiet reading rooms above buzzing immigrant services floors embody Quebec cultural policy.
grande_bibliotheque_banq.significance: Free civic amenity anchoring east-end student life.

orange_julep_gibeau.description: Glowing orange dome, classic car meets, and sweet creamy beverage define Montreal summer nights.
facts: Gibeau family recipe milkshake predates franchise aesthetic.
Highway Décarie trench curves around the parking moat.
orange_julep_gibeau.history: Automobile suburb kitsch survived real estate pressure as heritage symbol.
orange_julep_gibeau.significance: Quebec nostalgia meme stronger than any chain sign.

stade_uniprix.address: 285 Dupuis Avenue, Jarry Park (now IGA Stadium)
stade_uniprix.year_built: 1996 tennis stadium replaces Expos ballpark infill
stade_uniprix.architecture_style: Open tennis bowl with temporary seating decks
stade_uniprix.description: ATP/WTA National Bank Open practice courts sprawl toward community ball diamonds.
facts: Expos played 1969–1976 in prior Jarry grandstand before Olympic Stadium.
Renamed through corporate tennis sponsorship cycles.
stade_uniprix.history: Minor-league ball nostalgia meets francophone tennis fandom.
stade_uniprix.significance: Premier summer tennis on STM-reachable neighbourhood park land.

mccord_museum_montreal.address: 690 Sherbrooke Street West, McGill ghetto edge
mccord_museum_montreal.year_built: 1921 David McCord gift building; Stewart wing 2013
mccord_museum_montreal.architecture_style: Neoclassical museum facade; contemporary glass bridge
mccord_museum_montreal.description: McCord Stewart stitches Anglophone documentary photography with Indigenous collections partnerships.
facts: William Notman photographic archive anchors 19th-century visual history.
Stewart costume and decorative arts collections merged 2021 naming.
mccord_museum_montreal.history: Philanthropic accumulation of Montreal material culture turned public institution.
mccord_museum_montreal.significance: Critical English-Montreal story told beside francophone national museums.

cimetiere_notre_dame_neiges_chapel.description: Funeral liturgy hub for Canada's largest Catholic cemetery; winter heating bays comfort mourners.
facts: Heated roads and chapels battle northern freeze-thaw burial logistics.
Sits upslope with views toward downtown steel.
cimetiere_notre_dame_neiges_chapel.history: Postwar liturgical reform simplified ornament while processing volume exploded.
cimetiere_notre_dame_neiges_chapel.significance: Threshold architecture between city grid and necropolis lawn.

ile_sainte_helene_jean_drapeau.architecture_style: Island park with British fort ruins and fair relics
ile_sainte_helene_jean_drapeau.description: Picnic lawns overlook Jacques-Cartier Bridge; Stewart Museum interprets garrison history.
facts: La Ronde amusement park leases provincial fairground footprint.
Named for navigator's mother, not the British nurse famous elsewhere.
ile_sainte_helene_jean_drapeau.history: Military redoubt became world's fair playground then electro youth culture.
ile_sainte_helene_jean_drapeau.significance: Green punctuation in mid-river separating city from South Shore.

mary_queen_of_the_world_cathedral.address: 1085 Rue de la Cathédrale, Montréal, QC
mary_queen_of_the_world_cathedral.description: A monumental Catholic cathedral tucked behind modern towers; the interior feels surprisingly Roman in scale.
facts: Known for its large dome and rich interior decoration.
Sits close to central stations and downtown walkways.
mary_queen_of_the_world_cathedral.history: Built as a statement of Catholic presence and urban confidence in a rapidly growing city.
mary_queen_of_the_world_cathedral.significance: Second cathedral of Montreal and a key downtown sacred landmark.

notre_dame_de_bon_secours_chapel.architecture_style: Small historic chapel and pilgrimage site
notre_dame_de_bon_secours_chapel.description: A small chapel near the river, tied to sailors and early colonial devotion; a compact stop with deep history.
facts: Often associated with maritime protection traditions.
Close to Bonsecours Market and Old Port walks.
notre_dame_de_bon_secours_chapel.history: Built on older foundations; associated with New France religious life and waterfront communities.
notre_dame_de_bon_secours_chapel.significance: One of Old Montreal’s most historically resonant chapels.

christ_church_cathedral_montreal.description: A cathedral embedded into a modern commercial complex; the contrast between stone nave and retail circulation is uniquely Montreal.
facts: Connected to the underground city network.
Known for its stained glass and choir tradition.
christ_church_cathedral_montreal.history: Anglican establishment built downtown as the city’s English-speaking institutions expanded.
christ_church_cathedral_montreal.significance: A distinctive example of heritage integration into modern downtown life.

st_james_cathedral_montreal.architecture_style: Gothic Revival church with later updates
st_james_cathedral_montreal.description: A broad city church on a commercial street; a good break during downtown walks between museums and stations.
facts: Often hosts concerts and community events.
Central location makes it an easy detour.
st_james_cathedral_montreal.history: Parish development followed downtown’s westward growth and changing demographics.
st_james_cathedral_montreal.significance: One of downtown’s key Catholic parish churches outside the Old Montreal core.

saint_pierre_apotre_montreal.description: A warm, neighborhood-scale church near the Village; it feels more local than the headline basilicas.
facts: Close to Quartier des spectacles edges.
Distinct from Old Montreal monumental churches.
saint_pierre_apotre_montreal.history: Served growing working-class districts as the city expanded beyond the old walls.
saint_pierre_apotre_montreal.significance: A lived-in parish that rounds out Montreal’s sacred geography.
